heroLead: StackMe is a set of tools for working with data. Dataset generation, file analysis, autotests and more. Everything runs in the browser - no install, no sign-up required to try.
heroStatement: The system is designed so you don't have to take our word for it. Just check the code.
block1Title: Why it's free - and will it stay that way
block1p1: Because it's the right thing to do. We believe there should be more free tools accessible to everyone - and we run on enthusiasm and donations as a deliberate position, not a temporary measure.
block1p2: Everything we launched as free will stay free forever. Paid features will only appear for extra convenience: project storage, history, multi-project organisation. Core functionality - always free.
block2Title: What happens if we shut down
block2p1: All code is open on GitHub. Fork the repo and deploy it yourself - one command via Docker Compose. This isn't a fallback plan,
block2accent1: it's a guarantee built into the architecture from day one.
block2p2: Tools like AnalyzeMe run directly in your browser - data never leaves your device. We build on open technologies - FastAPI, React, DuckDB - intentionally, so anyone can run their own copy without depending on our infrastructure.
block3p1: We care about privacy. Your data is yours only - we never use it or share it with third parties. Full stop.
block3p2: Monetisation in our products is always transparent and voluntary.
block3accent: Monetisation in our products is always transparent and voluntary.
block3p2rest: No hidden subscriptions, fake countdown timers, or dark UI patterns - and there never will be. We don't profit from manipulation. It's not interesting to us, and it's not who we are.
block4Title: Who's behind this and is it serious
block4p1: A small team that believes: if you can make something useful and free - make it. We're not a corporation or a VC-backed startup, which is exactly why we don't need to squeeze the maximum out of you.
block4p2: The project is alive: open Roadmap, public backlog, discussions inside each tool.
block4accent: Vote for features, propose your own, write to us directly.
block5p1: Each tool solves a specific problem - open it and try without sign-up, without onboarding, without a form. If it fits - add it to your stack.
block5accent: People who come back do so because it's convenient,
block5p2rest: not because we trapped them. If the product doesn't suit you - that's okay, we wish you well.
block6p1: We welcome contributors, partnerships and people with ideas. Reach out via GitHub or our community.
block6accent: There should be more good in the world - and we believe that.

cards.freeLine: Free - no sign-up, no paywall, nothing to upgrade.
cards.locateSubtitle: Static locator audit for Playwright & Cypress
cards.locateNeed: you have to judge a test suite you didn't write - a client or legacy repo, a project you're joining blind, or work you're scoping before you commit. You get a fast read on how solid or fragile the locators are, without running a thing. (Works on your own suite too, when UI changes keep breaking tests.)
cards.locateGet: every locator graded by shape (fragile / stable / context / dynamic), the reason why, and duplicate selectors grouped so you fix one and close many. Read statically - by default, nothing leaves your browser.
cards.locateNot: you want a pass/fail score or an auto-fix. LocateMe flags and explains, you decide - a first pass, not a verdict.
cards.locateHonest: we never run your tests and never rewrite your code.
cards.forgeDesc: Schema-defined data with injected nulls, dupes, and outliers. Export or pipe into AnalyzeMe.
cards.analyzeDesc: Missing values, dupes, and outliers, entirely in your browser. No server, no uploads, no logs.
